steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS W/O CONTRAST)

ensemble.other_hypothesis: Malignancy-related process (metastatic breast cancer involving abdomen/retroperitoneum, biliary obstruction, or peritoneal involvement); chemotherapy-induced injury; renal pathology contributing to acute-on-chronic CKD; bowel obstruction or ileus
ensemble.information_gap: The anatomic source and extent of the intra-abdominal pathology driving the elevated lipase, epigastric pain, acute-on-chronic kidney injury, and thrombocytopenia is unknown — no prior imaging of the abdomen/pelvis has been performed in this encounter.
ensemble.expected_finding: Pancreatic edema, peripancreatic fat stranding, or ductal dilation consistent with pancreatitis; alternatively, hepatic or peritoneal metastases, biliary pathology, retroperitoneal adenopathy, or a renal structural abnormality explaining the acute-on-chronic CKD
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: With a lipase of 152 (>2.5× ULN), epigastric pain worsened by eating, acute-on-chronic kidney injury, thrombocytopenia, and active HER2+ breast cancer on chemotherapy — all without any prior abdominal imaging in this visit — a non-contrast CT abdomen/pelvis is the appropriate first-line cross-sectional study to simultaneously evaluate for pancreatitis, malignant complications, and structural renal disease; contrast is appropriately withheld given the severely reduced eGFR of 13–16.
grounding: Lipase: 152.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-60.0)
HPI: one week of epigastric non-radiating abd pain exacerbated by eating/drinking
HPI: new acute on chronic CKD, new BUN/Cr 68/3.55
Creatinine: 3.5 mg/dL (ref 0.4-1.1); Estimated GFR = 13 if non African-American
Platelet Count: 118.0 K/uL (ref 150.0-400.0)
PMH: Breast cancer ER-/PR-/HER2+NEU+; cycle 2 neoadjuvant chemotherapy using pertuzumab/trastuzumab/docetaxel
PMH: admitted to ___ with febrile neutropenia, abdominal pain; CT A/P negative; thought to have possible gastritis
Radiology Studies: None (in ER)
Alkaline Phosphatase: 193.0 IU/L (ref 35.0-105.0)
Albumin: 3.3 g/dL (ref 3.5-5.2)
ensemble.reasoning: This patient has a lipase more than twice the upper limit of normal alongside epigastric pain worsened by eating, making pancreatitis the leading concern, but her active HER2+ breast cancer on chemotherapy raises the additional possibility of malignant abdominal involvement (hepatic metastases, peritoneal disease, biliary obstruction) that could explain the markedly elevated alkaline phosphatase, hypoalbuminemia, and thrombocytopenia. The acute-on-chronic kidney injury with eGFR of 13 precludes iodinated contrast, so a non-contrast CT is the safest way to simultaneously evaluate pancreatic morphology, hepatobiliary structures, retroperitoneum, and renal anatomy without risking further nephrotoxicity.

verification.actual_finding: CT abdomen and pelvis (non-contrast) showed a completely normal pancreas without edema, ductal dilation, or peripancreatic stranding, normal kidneys without structural abnormality, no biliary pathology, no hepatic lesions, and no retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.
verification.relation_to_expectation: None of the expected findings — pancreatitis, biliary pathology, hepatic or peritoneal metastases, retroperitoneal adenopathy, or renal structural abnormality — were identified, leaving the anatomic source of the clinical syndrome unexplained by this imaging.
